mechanism_narrative: Continued increases in FEDFUNDS raise the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and tighten financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the Fed remains in an active hiking cycle.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rising from near-zero through mid-2022 as Fed combats inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or SPX posts positive returns in >55% of months with rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE consistent with a Sahm-rule-style trigger signals the onset of recessionary labor market deterioration, which historically precedes sustained equity drawdowns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp above its 3-month prior low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% near historic lows as of 2022-08
failure_condition: When the 0.3pp UNRATE trigger fires, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of historical triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When nominal retail sales growth (RSAFS) fails to outpace CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL), real consumer demand is contracting even as headline sales appear stable, foreshadowing weaker corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: 3-month RSAFS growth rate falls below 3-month CPIAUCSL growth rate
regime_hint: high inflation with decelerating real consumption
failure_condition: Real retail sales deceleration (RSAFS growth minus CPI growth negative) fails to predict negative 20d SPX returns with Rank IC < 0.03, or hit rate of negative returns falls below 50% over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion following aggressive monetary tightening, a deceleration in INDPRO month-over-month growth signals weakening industrial output and impending earnings compression, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.3648 with growth momentum slowing amid Fed tightening
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no significant negative relationship (Rank IC < 0.03) with INDPRO deceleration episodes over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns occur in >55% of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 8%, markets price in an accelerated and prolonged Fed tightening path, causing rapid equity repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL YoY inflation running above 8% through mid-2022
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following CPI prints above the 8% YoY threshold are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects resilient labor demand and supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, contributing to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month under normal conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152536 thousand persons showing continued monthly job gains in 2022
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly growth and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or negative returns occur in more than 50% of months with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rising FEDFUNDS combined with declining HOUST indicates that higher mortgage rates are actively destroying housing demand, a leading indicator of broader consumer retrenchment that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for 3 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS rises
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1559 thousand units amid rapid FEDFUNDS increases in 2022
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing slowdown
failure_condition: When both HOUST decline and FEDFUNDS rise jointly trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During an active monetary tightening regime, a deceleration in quarterly GDPC1 growth signals that restrictive policy is beginning to bite into real output, prompting a swift negative repricing of equities within the first week after the release becomes public knowledge.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19681.682 billion chained dollars with growth momentum slowing through 2022
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following GDPC1 growth deceleration prints are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.
